items.title: Polls close in Nepean by-election in Victoria

children.content: The Liberal Party seeks to retain the seat vacated by former Victorian deputy Liberal leader Sam Groth.

articles.title: Why the Victorian Liberals can't afford to lose this weekend's by-election

children.content: With no Labor candidate contesting this weekend's Nepean by-election, it should be a cakewalk for the Victorian Liberals. But we are no longer in a normal political world.

image.altText: A woman smiles as she arrives to campaign on the hustings.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a man with a cap saying "One Nation", a man in a navy cap and a woman in pink cap, all smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a man with a cap saying "One Nation", a man in a navy cap and a woman in pink cap, all smiling.

image.altText: A graphic of a hand placing a ballot inside a voting box

items.title: Man shot at and venues set on fire in latest Melbourne attacks

children.content: A man has been shot at during an aggravated home invasion, and a pub and restaurant were set on fire overnight in Melbourne.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Man airlifted with life-threatening injuries after caravan park brawl

children.content: A man has been airlifted to hospital with life-threatening injuries after being stabbed during a wild brawl at a caravan park in a small Victorian town.

image.altText: Four police officers in high-vis uniforms, with their backs to the camera.

items.title: Brisbane Lions coach slams 'hero' parent after whiteboard leak

children.content: Chris Fagan has expressed his disappointment after the leaking of confidential Brisbane Lions notes on Essendon players.

image.altText: An image of whiteboard appeaqringg to show Lions' analysis of Bombers players.

items.title: Towering mark ignites Blues-Saints clash after Tigers get on the board

children.content: Matthew Carroll soars for Carlton in a spiteful clash with St Kilda, after Richmond snapped a 12-game winless run with victory against West Coast. Follow live.

image.altText: An AFL player in dark blue takes a mark over the top of players in red, white and black stripes

items.title: Second of two new Spirit of Tasmania ferries arrives in Geelong

children.content: Following a seven-week journey from Scotland, the Spirit of Tasmania V has arrived in Geelong. It will undergo gantry testing tomorrow before being temporarily relocated to Station Pier in Melbourne.

image.altText: A large red and white ferry with SPIRIT OF TASMANIA on it sailing through a bay.

items.title: The four women who left 'Australia Street' to try and leave Syria

children.content: Four Australian women, their children and grandchildren are stuck in limbo in Syria after leaving a refugee camp for family members of suspected Islamic State fighters. This is what we know about them.

image.altText: A group of women wearing hijabs and face masks, accompanied by a group of children.

items.title: Police pulled him over on his farm. He was tasered 30 seconds later

children.content: Disturbing cases of people wrongly tasered by police on their farm and at school has raised questions over whether officers are too reliant on the devices.

gallery.altText: A composite image of  a man being threatened with a taser then being tasered by police at night.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of  a man being threatened with a taser then being tasered by police at night.

items.title: Engineering students launch rocket to 10,000 feet in Australian outback

children.content: Students with space industry aspirations successfully launch a rocket 10,000 feet in the air in regional Australia.

image.altText: A student built rocket launches in the desert.

items.title: Mernda residents have been waiting for a pool since 2004. It may not happen at all

children.content: A council in Melbourne's north-east says it may no longer be able to afford a long-awaited pool and aquatic centre.

image.altText: A steel ladder on the side of an outdoor pool.

items.title: As I sat for an Archibald entry, I tried to assume an air of dignity

children.content: What began as an awkward pose for the Archibald Prize became a meditation on why portrait painting still matters in the age of the iPhone.

image.altText: virginia Trioli in a blue shirt and pants with a fine white stripe has her arm over a man in a white shirt, a painting behind

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Polar blast and snow to follow record May heat over south-east Australia

children.content: Just weeks from winter, and south-east Australia is still enjoying summertime warmth.

image.altText: saftey beach in victoria

items.title: 1976 in music: The big albums turning 50 this year

children.content: The music industry is always in a state of flux, but what was happening in 1976 was akin to the dinosaurs looking up and seeing a meteor on the way.

gallery.altText: AC/DC pose for a band photo in 1976.

thumbnail.altText: AC/DC pose for a band photo in 1976.

avatar.alt: A profile image of a young man with short brown hair.

items.title: May temperature records fall across Victoria as warm weather continues

children.content: Weather records fall across the state, as a spell of summer-like conditions tips into the first day of May.

image.altText: Bollards by the waterfront in Geelong

items.title: Melbourne teacher accused of installing bathroom camera denied bail

children.content: Parents of children attending Kingswood Primary School filled the Moorabbin Magistrates Court on Friday, intending to see accused teacher Timothy Blamires.

image.altText: Timothy Blamires

items.title: 'No secrets': Alternative school's history of discipline in spotlight

children.content: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

image.altText: Faye Berryman and Philip O'Carroll in a sepia toned photo with deidentified children.

items.title: At a Melbourne hospital, nitrous oxide users are presenting with psychosis

children.content: Harms from nitrous oxide have been growing in Australia in recent years, with the substance linked to a growing number of accidental deaths, while a study from a Melbourne hospital shows abuse of the inhalant is associated with psychosis.

image.altText: two stills from a phone video showing discarded nitrous oxide cylinders in Melbourne

items.title: Thrift store chain denies there are bed bugs at popular Melbourne shop

children.content: Thrift store chain Savers Australia has denied there is evidence of bed bugs at their popular Brunswick shop, as allegations of an outbreak continue to circulate online.

items.title: Legal change promised after 'horrific' stabbing of paramedic

children.content: Ambulance Victoria and the paramedics union say some people who attack paramedics have escaped mandatory jail terms due to the law's definition of "on-duty".

image.altText: An Ambulance Victoria ambulance in front of an office building.

items.title: Hardwick fires back at Lyon's 'compromised draft' call

children.content: Gold Coast coach Damien Hardwick provides a spirited response to St Kilda counterpart Ross Lyon's comment about the AFL national draft.

image.altText: Damien Hardwick looks on during an AFL semifinal in 2025.

items.title: Plans for Dan Murphy's on Phillip Island spark fears about influx of retail chains

children.content: Dan Murphy's wants to move onto Phillip Island but residents want to protect their island's identity from growing retail chain development.

image.altText: Dan Murphy's logo on liquor store with blue sky

items.title: Remote health technology saving time and money for regional mums during pregnancy

children.content: Remote health technology is saving regional patients time and money, despite many Victorian hospitals still running on paper records.

image.altText: A woman folds baby clothes while smiling at the camera.

items.title: Search underway for Victorian hiker missing on Canadian island

children.content: Canadian police are searching for a Victorian woman who disappeared while hiking on an island on the country's east coast.

image.altText: A headshot of Denise Ann Williams

items.title: Melbourne and Sydney record large house price falls as growth slows across the country

children.content: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

image.altText: A new housing estate stretches as far as the eye can see.

items.title: Hundreds of child abuse files found on police killer Dezi Freeman's devices

children.content: Police killer Dezi Freeman was on the verge of having his secret stash of child abuse material discovered when he gunned down two officers outside his makeshift home.

gallery.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

thumbnail.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Victoria Police to double recruit intake to fill 1,500 vacancies

children.content: The police force is trying to fill a shortfall in frontline officers with crime in Victoria at a 10-year high.

image.altText: Four police officers in high-vis uniforms, with their backs to the camera.

items.title: Bottle shops awash with illicit booze as police probe arson attacks

children.content: The National Drug Research Institute found 30 per cent of bottle shops it audited across different areas of Melbourne and regional Victoria were stocking illicit alcohol.

image.altText: Spirit bottles in an alcohol shop.

items.title: Wine code of conduct details emerge as industry continues to shrink

children.content: Industry representatives welcome a new mandatory code of conduct that dictates how wineries treat wine grape growers, but it may be too late for some producers.

image.altText: Rows of grapevines lie in darkness as the yellow sun peeks over distant hills

items.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in school bathroom

children.content: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

image.altText: A man wearing glasses

items.title: VicGrid surveyors cancel access visit after farmgate protest makes them feel unsafe

children.content: Renewable planning body VicGrid has threatened a high-profile anti-transmission-line campaigner with enforcement action after government surveyors felt "hindered" from accessing his farm.

items.title: 'Free Palestine' party plans to funnel votes to Victorian conservatives

children.content: Victorian voters are being urged to look out for "misleading" party names, as right-wing activists seek to redirect progressive votes to conservative parties.

image.altText: Avi Yemeni

items.title: Major childcare operator reveals list of 40 centres to close

children.content: Victoria and Western Australia will be affected the most, with 12 centres shutting in each state. NSW, Queensland and South Australia will also have closures.

image.altText: An image of a young childs hand playing with plastic letters to spell the word goat.

items.title: Roadworks on hold as regional councils grapple with fuel prices

children.content: Regional roadworks and construction projects are being put on hold as Victorian councils tighten their budgets amid the international fuel crisis.

image.altText: A road sign of a rough road

items.title: Emotions run high at inquiry into Victorian bushfires

children.content: A parliamentary inquiry into the devastating bushfires across Victoria over the summer hears the emotional plea of those impacted.

image.altText: a white car has been completely burnt out by a fire
